intro: ByteBrew exposes a free tier while AppsFlyer starts at $0.05-0.07/install (Growth). AppsFlyer is positioned for mobile, while ByteBrew leans toward indie and mid-size mobile game studios needing free all-in-one analytics plus attribution. In practice, AppsFlyer — Mobile attribution and marketing analytics platform with deterministic matching, deep links, and Privacy Sandbox support. Free all-in-one mobile game growth platform with analytics, monetization tracking, remote config and attribution. AppsFlyer scores 5/5 on developer experience versus 4/5 for ByteBrew. ByteBrew rates 5/5 on transparency versus 3/5 for AppsFlyer. Listed strengths unique to AppsFlyer include mobile apps with paid acquisition, while ByteBrew lists free plan covers analytics, attribution and remote config on its side. Reach for AppsFlyer when you need mobile apps with paid acquisition; reach for ByteBrew when the job is free all-in-one analytics + attribution + remote config for mobile games. The honest trade-off: AppsFlyer is not built for web-only products, and ByteBrew is not the right call for large publishers with strict slas or non-game app verticals, so neither is a universal default.
quick_take: AppsFlyer is for mobile; ByteBrew is for indie and mid-size mobile game studios needing free all-in-one analytics plus attribution; decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose AppsFlyer if mobile and the $0.05-0.07/install (Growth) entry price fits your budget.
verdict_b: Choose ByteBrew if indie and mid-size mobile game studios needing free all-in-one analytics plus attribution and you need a free tier to validate fit.

faq.a: ByteBrew has a free tier while AppsFlyer starts at $0.05-0.07/install (Growth), so at the lowest end ByteBrew costs less. Above the free limits, per-unit rates decide the winner, and those aren't in the summary data here. Run the numbers on your expected volume.

faq.q: Can I migrate from AppsFlyer to ByteBrew?
faq.a: Technically you can move between any two tools in the same category, but effort varies: leaving AppsFlyer is moderate effort at medium lock-in (Medium — attribution data tied to to AppsFlyer ecosystem), and onboarding to ByteBrew involves medium lock-in (Mobile growth SDK). Plan for data export, re-integration, and a parallel-run window rather than a hard cutover.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, AppsFlyer or ByteBrew?
faq.a: On the 0-5 developer experience score in our data, AppsFlyer rates 5/5 while ByteBrew rates 4/5, giving AppsFlyer the edge. Scores compress a lot of variance though — SDK language coverage, error messages, and docs quality matter more than the number. Run a quick integration spike on both before trusting the gap.

faq.q: Is ByteBrew a good alternative to AppsFlyer?
faq.a: ByteBrew is an alternative in the same category, but its stated sweet spot is indie and mid-size mobile game studios needing free all-in-one analytics plus attribution, while AppsFlyer's is mobile. If your job matches AppsFlyer's profile more closely, switching to ByteBrew is a compromise. Use the best-fit match, not brand familiarity, to decide. Note: ByteBrew is explicitly not for large publishers with strict slas or non-game app verticals.
